runs.text: How much outsourced, supplier-addressable new-construction work exists?

QUESTION ANSWERED

runs.text: Navy new-construction budget  →  strip non-supplier and non-component scope  →  apply program-specific supplier coefficients  →  add only incremental AP and LLTM

text_body.plain_text:
METHOD FLOW
Navy new-construction budget  →  strip non-supplier and non-component scope  →  apply program-specific supplier coefficients  →  add only incremental AP and LLTM
Output:  average annual outsourced supplier TAM

runs.text: What share of outsourced TAM belongs to each work type?

What share of outsourced TAM belongs to each work type?

runs.text: FFATA and FSRS subaward history, parent-prime PIID scope, UEI and registry evidence, vendor names, NAICS-4 fallback, descriptions as QA.

runs.text: Historical subaward evidence  →  scope by parent-prime PIID  →  retain supplier component roles  →  assign each retained dollar one primary work-type home  →  dollar-weighted shares

text_body.plain_text:
METHOD FLOW
Historical subaward evidence  →  scope by parent-prime PIID  →  retain supplier component roles  →  assign each retained dollar one primary work-type home  →  dollar-weighted shares

runs.text: Work-type SAM  =  average annual outsourced supplier TAM
TAM is the fixed budget-derived pool; SAM does not re-size it. Reclassification moves the shares, not the pool.

text_body.plain_text:
Work-type SAM  =  average annual outsourced supplier TAM  ×  award-derived work-type share
TAM is the fixed budget-derived pool; SAM does not re-size it. Reclassification moves the shares, not the pool.

runs.text: TAM is built from Navy budget inputs; SAM mix shares come from historical award evidence.
TAM is the fixed dollar pool. Reclassification changes the slicing percentages, not the size of the pool.
Budget and award flows are lumpy, so outputs are normalized to average annual opportunity, not a single year.
Award records tie to DDG or submarine through parent-prime PIID scope, not hull-level work-order proof.
Program dollar outputs are summed. Coefficients, stream treatments, and mix evidence are not blended into one generic input.

text_body.plain_text:
Method notes
Two evidence universes: TAM is built from Navy budget inputs; SAM mix shares come from historical award evidence.
Fixed pool: TAM is the fixed dollar pool. Reclassification changes the slicing percentages, not the size of the pool.
Average annual basis: Budget and award flows are lumpy, so outputs are normalized to average annual opportunity, not a single year.
Attribution basis: Award records tie to DDG or submarine through parent-prime PIID scope, not hull-level work-order proof.
Output discipline: Program dollar outputs are summed. Coefficients, stream treatments, and mix evidence are not blended into one generic input.

runs.text: Award data determines mix, not market size. Scenario views overlap and should not be added. The model sizes TAM and SAM opportunity only, with no SOM, capture rate, win probability, pricing haircut, or revenue conversion.

text_body.plain_text: Guardrails:  Award data determines mix, not market size. Scenario views overlap and should not be added. The model sizes TAM and SAM opportunity only, with no SOM, capture rate, win probability, pricing haircut, or revenue conversion.

runs.text: Sources: (1) Navy SCN and P-5c shipbuilding budget justification, FY2022–FY2027; (2) FFATA and FSRS subaward records, FY2017–FY2026; (3) DoD place-of-performance and parent-prime PIID attribution evidence
